summary: Recent updates to the 2D graphics subsystem in Firefox have focused on improving font handling and optimizing performance in multi-GPU systems. Notably, the **addition of autorelease pools for font initialization on macOS** aims to enhance startup efficiency, while changes to font metrics calculation ensure better rendering for varied scripts, including niche characters like the Mongolian Vowel Separator. Additionally, the enhancements in multi-GPU support and video processing, alongside optimizations in text frame management, ensure that graphics-heavy applications run more smoothly across different hardware setups. Overall, these adjustments aim to provide users with a more efficient and visually accurate browsing experience.
